title: Ampelocissus ochracea
taxonomy: Ampelocissus ochracea is classified within the family Vitaceae, commonly known as the grape family. This plant is a species of flowering plant native to tropical Africa. The classification of Ampelocissus ochracea further breaks down into the kingdom Plantae, and the order Vitales. It is a woody climber with small, greenish-yellow flowers. The species name ochracea refers to the plant's distinctive yellowish or orange coloration.
uses: Ampelocissus ochracea, a plant species found in various parts of the world, is reported to have several medicinal uses. Traditionally, its extracts have been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The plant is believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to its potential health benefits.
